lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Mumblelandlubbermumblemumble...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: What right have ye to be so happy, landlubber?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it is quite a nice day.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Aye, that it is.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Will ye forgive an old sailor his temper [lad/lass/kid]? I'm in dire straits indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the problem? I may be able to help.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: I dunno, [lad/lass/kid], I need a pirate to help me with my problem, but none will sail with me.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well, ye look like ye might have the right stuff, tell me would ye like te be a pirate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, it sounds quite dangerous.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Aye, that it be. Come back when yer liver ain't so lily-like, [lad/lass/kid].

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: If yer after a lift go to the ship and I'll take ye where ye want to go.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: I suppose ye'll be wantin' a lift while yer here, right [lad/lass/kid]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cap'n, I lost that book you gave me.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Ye'll notice I'm not fallin' to the floor in shock right now. Look, if I gives ye another copy will ye keep a better eye on it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aye Cap'n!

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Then here, have this other copy I dug up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes Cap'n, I'd like a lift. If you aren't busy.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: No, I should be gettin' back anyway. Come on [lad/lass/kid], let's be off.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Nothin' I won't put on hold. Come on [lad/lass/kid], let's be off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, Cap'n.

lines.speaker: Bill Teach
lines.text: Well, come see me if ye ever do.
